terms.definition: Two parallel metal rails laid on the ground on which trains run. The rails are fixed to wooden or concrete sleepers.
terms.exampleUsage: The train moved smoothly along the railway track.

terms.definition: A raised flat area at a railway station where passengers wait for and get on or off trains.
terms.exampleUsage: We waited on platform number 3 for our train to arrive.

terms.definition: A passage dug through a hill or mountain so that trains can pass through without going over it.
terms.exampleUsage: The train went through a long dark tunnel in the mountains.

terms.definition: A structure built over a river, valley, or road so that trains or people can cross safely from one side to the other.
terms.exampleUsage: The train crossed a bridge over the wide river.

terms.definition: A device with coloured lights (red, yellow, green) that tells the train driver whether to stop, slow down, or go ahead safely.
terms.exampleUsage: The train stopped because the signal was red.

terms.definition: The scenery or view of an area of land, including hills, fields, rivers, trees, and buildings that you can see.
terms.exampleUsage: The landscape changed from green fields to dry brown hills as the train moved south.

terms.definition: A person who sells things. At railway stations, vendors sell tea, snacks, water, books, and other items to passengers.
terms.exampleUsage: The vendor called out 'Chai, chai!' as he walked through the train.

terms.definition: A person at a railway station who helps passengers carry their heavy luggage for a fee. Also called a coolie.
terms.exampleUsage: The porter carried our heavy bags from the platform to the taxi stand.

terms.definition: A place where a road crosses a railway track at the same level. Gates close the road when a train is coming.
terms.exampleUsage: We waited at the level crossing for the train to pass before the gates opened.

terms.definition: A separate section or coach of a train where passengers sit or sleep during their journey.
terms.exampleUsage: Our seats were in compartment number 5 of the train.
